revision.comment: 新しいページ：塚穴古墳（三重県志摩市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 塚穴古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = 志島4号墳 |所属 = [[志島古墳群]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[志摩市]]阿児町志島234ほか |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 18|緯度秒 = 39.80 |経度度 = 136|経度分 = 53|経度秒 = 20.00 |ISO = JP-24 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径20m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]（高倉山型） |出土品 = 銅鋺・金銅製品・耳環・武器・馬具・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 志摩市指定史跡「志島古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Mie |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 塚穴古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''塚穴古墳'''（つかあなこふん、志島4号墳）は、[[三重県]][[志摩市]]阿児町志島にある古墳。[[志島古墳群]]（志摩市指定史跡）を構成する古墳の1つ。形状は[[円墳]]。
== 概要 == 三重県東部、[[志摩半島]]先端部の安乗崎・大王崎の間、緩やかに東に突出する海食台地の、北東端の段崖上に築造された古墳である。[[2012年|2012]]-[[2014年]]度（[[平成]]24-26年度）に発掘調査が実施されている。
墳形は円形で、直径約20メートルを測る。墳丘周囲には周溝が巡らされる{{Sfn|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}。ただし、北側では周溝は別の円弧を描いており、削平された5号墳と周溝を共有した可能性がある{{Sfn|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南南西方向に開口する。石室全長10.6メートルを測る大型石室で、[[伊勢市]]の[[高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|高倉山古墳]]に類似する高倉山型石室になる。石室内は盗掘に遭っているが、調査では銅鋺・金銅製品のほか耳環・武器・馬具・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]など多数の副葬品が検出されている。築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]中葉頃と推定される{{Sfn|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}。なお、石室下層では古墳時代中期の[[5世紀]]前半（TK216型式期）の須恵器・土師器が検出されており、中期古墳を壊して築造された可能性があるとして注目される。
古墳域は[[1971年]]（[[昭和]]46年）に阿児町指定史跡（現在は志摩市指定史跡）に指定されている（史跡「志島古墳群」のうち）。
== 遺跡歴 == * [[1971年]]（昭和46年）3月31日、阿児町指定史跡に指定（史跡「志島古墳群」のうち、現在は志摩市指定史跡）。 * 石室実測調査（村上喜雄、[[1976年]]に報告）。 * 石室実測調査（[[関西大学]]文学部考古学研究室、[[1992年]]に報告）。 * [[2012年|2012]]-[[2014年]]度（[[平成]]24-26年度）、発掘調査（志摩市教育委員会、[[2018年]]に概要報告）{{Sfn|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}。 ** 2012年度（平成24年度）、墳丘測量・発掘調査：第1次調査。 ** 2013年度（平成25年度）、石室前庭部・石室内発掘調査：第2次調査。 ** 2014年度（平成26年度）、墳丘断割・周溝確認調査：第3次調査。
== 埋葬施設 == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南南西方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}。 * 石室全長：10.8メートル * 玄室：長さ7.7メートル、幅2.2メートル、高さ2.3メートル * 羨道：幅1.4メートル 玄室の奥壁では、最下段に大石2石を置いた上に石を積み上げるが、奥壁と側壁の境界はL字状の石を架け渡すなど不明瞭である。天井石は6石で、奥壁から2石目を最高所として開口部に向かって下がる{{Sfn|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}。石室の形態としては、長大な玄室・短小な羨道・断面弧状の玄室天井・両袖の退化などの点で[[高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|高倉山古墳]]（[[伊勢市]]）・小金3号墳（[[明和町 (三重県)|明和町]]）などと同様の特徴を有しており、「高倉山型石室」と捉えられる。
石室内は盗掘に遭っているが、石室内の調査では銅鋺・蛇尾・鋳造鈴・鍛造鈴・板状金銅製品・花形飾金具のほか、耳環・玉（空玉・勾玉・丸玉・小玉・トンボ玉）・刀・鏃・弓飾金具・刀子・刀子装具・銀装鉄釘・鉄釘・馬具（雲珠・双脚飾鋲・飾金具・鉸具・鉄製壺鐙・兵庫鎖）・須恵器（坏・高坏・平瓶・𤭯・甕）・土師器（坏・甕）などが検出されている。また石室内の断ち割り調査では、下層から古墳時代中期の須恵器（𤭯2・壺1）・土師器（高坏5）が検出されており、築造以前に中期古墳が存在した可能性が指摘される{{Sfn|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}。 <gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|開口部 </gallery>
== 関連施設 == * [[志摩市歴史民俗資料館]]（志摩市磯部町迫間）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=志島古墳群|title=三重県史|volume=資料編 考古1|publisher=三重県|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=塚穴古墳（志島古墳群4号墳）発掘調査概報|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63081|title=平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|series=志摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告5|publisher=志摩市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/material/files/group/57/shimashinobunkazai.pdf 志摩市の文化財]}}|publisher=志摩市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|志摩市の文化財|2018}}}} - リンクは志摩市ホームページ。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=志島古墳群|title=三重県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]24|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490247|ref={{Harvid|志島古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}}}
== 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=村上喜雄|title=志摩の横穴式石室|date=1976|journal=郷土志摩|publisher=志摩郷土会|volume=|number=49|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[関西大学]]文学部考古学研究室|author=|year=1992|chapter=阿児町志島古墳群の調査|title=紀伊半島の文化史的研究|volume=考古学編|series=関西大学文学部考古学研究第6冊|publisher=清文堂出版|isbn=|ref=}}

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 塚穴古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = 志島4号墳 |所属 = [[志島古墳群]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[志摩市]]阿児町志島234ほか |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 18|緯度秒 = 39.80 |経度度 = 136|経度分 = 53|経度秒 = 20.00 |ISO = JP-24 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径20m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]（高倉山型） |出土品 = 銅鋺・金銅製品・耳環・武器・馬具・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 志摩市指定史跡「志島古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Mie |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 塚穴古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''塚穴古墳'''（つかあなこふん、志島4号墳）は、[[三重県]][[志摩市]]阿児町志島にある古墳。[[志島古墳群]]（志摩市指定史跡）を構成する古墳の1つ。形状は[[円墳]]。

== 埋葬施設 == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南南西方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}。 * 石室全長：10.8メートル * 玄室：長さ7.7メートル、幅2.2メートル、高さ2.3メートル * 羨道：幅1.4メートル 玄室の奥壁では、最下段に大石2石を置いた上に石を積み上げるが、奥壁と側壁の境界はL字状の石を架け渡すなど不明瞭である。天井石は6石で、奥壁から2石目を最高所として開口部に向かって下がる{{Sfn|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}。石室の形態としては、長大な玄室・短小な羨道・断面弧状の玄室天井・両袖の退化などの点で[[高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|高倉山古墳]]（[[伊勢市]]）・小金3号墳（[[明和町 (三重県)|明和町]]）などと同様の特徴を有しており、「高倉山型石室」と捉えられる。

revision.text:
{{Japanese burial mounds |Name = Tsukaana Kofun |Image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |Alternate Name = Shima No. 4 Kofun |Group = [[Shima Burial Mounds]] |Location = [[Shima, Mie|Shima]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan |Latitude Degrees = 34|Latitude Minutes = 18|Latitude Seconds = 39.80 |Longitude Degrees = 136|Longitude Minutes = 53|Longitude Seconds = 20.00 |ISO = JP-24 |Type = [[Round Kofun]] |Size = Diameter 20m |Burial Facility = Dual entrance [[Horizontal stone chamber]] (Takakurayama type) |Artifacts = Bronze bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse equipment, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] |Satellite Tombs = |Construction Date = Mid-7th century |Buried Individual = |Mausoleum = |Historical Site = Included in Shima City-designated historical site "Shima Burial Mounds" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |Map = Japan Mie |Icon = Round Kofun |Label = Tsukaana Kofun |Label Position = bottom }}
'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in [[Shima, Mie|Shima]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Shima Burial Mounds]], a group of burial mounds designated as a Shima City historical site. The tomb is round in shape.
== Overview == Situated on the eastern tip of a marine-eroded plateau between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō on the [[Shima Peninsula]], the Tsukaana Kofun was built along a cliff edge facing northeast. Archaeological excavations were conducted between [[2012]] and [[2014]].
The tomb measures approximately 20 meters in diameter, with a surrounding moat. On its northern side, the moat forms a separate arc, suggesting that it may have been shared with a now-destroyed No. 5 Kofun. The burial chamber is a dual-entrance [[horizontal stone chamber]] facing south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in length, it is categorized as a Takakurayama-type stone chamber, similar to the [[Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|Takakurayama Kofun]] in [[Ise, Mie|Ise]]. Despite evidence of looting, artifacts such as bronze bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse gear, [[Sue ware]], and [[Haji ware]] were uncovered. The tomb is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the [[Kofun period]]'s terminal phase.
Significantly, artifacts from the early 5th century were discovered beneath the chamber floor, suggesting that the mound may have been built over an earlier kofun.
The site was designated as a historical site in [[1971]].
== Excavation History == * [[1971]] (Showa 46): Designated as a historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * [[1976]]: Detailed measurement of the chamber reported by Yoshio Murakami. * [[1992]]: Chamber measurements reported by the Archaeology Department of [[Kansai University]]. * [[2012–2014]]: Excavations conducted by the Shima City Board of Education. * 2012: Mound survey and initial excavation (1st phase). * 2013: Forecourt and interior excavation (2nd phase). * 2014: Mound sectioning and moat survey (3rd phase).
== Burial Chamber == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan view of the stone chamber}}]]
The burial chamber features a dual-entrance [[horizontal stone chamber]] with a south-southwest opening. Dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Burial chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber’s rear wall is constructed with large stones, with L-shaped stones bridging the walls and ceiling. The ceiling comprises six stones, sloping downward from the rear toward the entrance. The structure is classified as a "Takakurayama-type chamber," sharing traits with the [[Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|Takakurayama Kofun]] and Kogane No. 3 Kofun in [[Meiwa, Mie|Meiwa]].
Artifacts discovered include bronze bowls, gilt-bronze items, jewelry, weapons, horse gear, and pottery. Evidence of an earlier 5th-century kofun beneath the chamber includes [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]].
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (view toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
== Related Facilities == * [[Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum]] (Isobe-cho, Shima)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for the article}} * Local Government Publications: * {{Cite book|title=Shima Burial Mounds|year=2005|publisher=Mie Prefecture|chapter=Shima Burial Mounds|series=Mie Prefecture History: Archaeology Vol. 1|ref={{Harvid|Shima Burial Mounds (Mie History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Tsukaana Kofun (Shima Burial Mound No. 4) Excavation Report|year=2018|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63081|publisher=Shima City Board of Education|series=Shima City Archaeological Research Reports Vol. 5|ref={{Harvid|Shima Archaeological Reports|2018}}}}. * {{Cite book|title=Shima City's Cultural Properties|year=2018|url=https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/material/files/group/57/shimashinobunkazai.pdf|publisher=Shima City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Shima Cultural Properties|2018}}}}. * Encyclopedias: * {{Cite book|title=Mie Prefecture Place Names|year=1983|series=Japanese Historical Place Names Series Vol. 24|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Mie Prefecture Place Names (Heibonsha)|1983}}}}.
== Related Literature == {{small|Not used in article creation}} * {{Cite journal|author=Yoshio Murakami|title=Shima’s Horizontal Stone Chambers|date=1976|journal=Shima Local History|publisher=Shima Historical Society}} * {{Cite book|title=Archaeological Research on the Cultural History of the Kii Peninsula|year=1992|series=Archaeological Research Vol. 6|publisher=Seibundo Publishing|ref=}}.
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Tsukaana Kofun (Shima)}} * [[Shima Burial Mounds]]

{{Japanese burial mounds |Name = Tsukaana Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] |Caption = Mound and stone chamber entrance |Alternate Name = Shima No. 4 Kofun |Group = [[Shima Burial Mounds]] |Location = [[Shima, Mie|Shima]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan |Latitude Degrees = 34|Latitude Minutes = 18|Latitude Seconds = 39.80 |Longitude Degrees = 136|Longitude Minutes = 53|Longitude Seconds = 20.00 |ISO = JP-24 |Type = [[Round Kofun]] |Size = Diameter 20m |Burial Facility = Dual entrance [[Horizontal stone chamber]] (Takakurayama type) |Artifacts = Bronze bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse equipment, [[Sue ware]], [[Haji ware]] |Satellite Tombs = |Construction Date = Mid-7th century |Buried Individual = |Mausoleum = |Historical Site = Included in Shima City-designated historical site "Shima Burial Mounds" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |Map = Japan Mie |Icon = Round Kofun |Label = Tsukaana Kofun |Label Position = bottom }}
'''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in [[Shima, Mie|Shima]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[Shima Burial Mounds]], a group of burial mounds designated as a Shima City historical site. The tomb is round in shape.
== Overview == Situated on the eastern tip of a marine-eroded plateau between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō on the [[Shima Peninsula]], the Tsukaana Kofun was built along a cliff edge facing northeast. Archaeological excavations were conducted between [[2012]] and [[2014]].
The tomb measures approximately 20 meters in diameter, with a surrounding moat. On its northern side, the moat forms a separate arc, suggesting that it may have been shared with a now-destroyed No. 5 Kofun. The burial chamber is a dual-entrance [[horizontal stone chamber]] facing south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in length, it is categorized as a Takakurayama-type stone chamber, similar to the [[Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|Takakurayama Kofun]] in [[Ise, Mie|Ise]]. Despite evidence of looting, artifacts such as bronze bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse gear, [[Sue ware]], and [[Haji ware]] were uncovered. The tomb is estimated to date to the mid-7th century during the [[Kofun period]]'s terminal phase.
Significantly, artifacts from the early 5th century were discovered beneath the chamber floor, suggesting that the mound may have been built over an earlier kofun.
The site was designated as a historical site in [[1971]].
== Excavation History == * [[1971]] (Showa 46): Designated as a historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * [[1976]]: Detailed measurement of the chamber reported by Yoshio Murakami. * [[1992]]: Chamber measurements reported by the Archaeology Department of [[Kansai University]]. * [[2012–2014]]: Excavations conducted by the Shima City Board of Education. * 2012: Mound survey and initial excavation (1st phase). * 2013: Forecourt and interior excavation (2nd phase). * 2014: Mound sectioning and moat survey (3rd phase).
== Burial Chamber == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Plan view of the stone chamber}}]]
The burial chamber features a dual-entrance [[horizontal stone chamber]] with a south-southwest opening. Dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Burial chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber’s rear wall is constructed with large stones, with L-shaped stones bridging the walls and ceiling. The ceiling comprises six stones, sloping downward from the rear toward the entrance. The structure is classified as a "Takakurayama-type chamber," sharing traits with the [[Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|Takakurayama Kofun]] and Kogane No. 3 Kofun in [[Meiwa, Mie|Meiwa]].
Artifacts discovered include bronze bowls, gilt-bronze items, jewelry, weapons, horse gear, and pottery. Evidence of an earlier 5th-century kofun beneath the chamber includes [[Sue ware]] and [[Haji ware]].
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main burial chamber (view toward rear wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main burial chamber (view toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (view toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
== Related Facilities == * [[Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum]] (Isobe-cho, Shima)

{{Kofun in Japan | Name = Tsukaana Kofun | Image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | Image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate Name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | Type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Kofun in Japan | Name = Tsukaana Kofun | Image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | Image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate Name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | Type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Kofun in Japan | Name = Tsukaana Kofun | Image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | Image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate Name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | Type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Kofun in Japan | Name = Tsukaana Kofun | Image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | Image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate Name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | Type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.

Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."

The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.

{{Kofun in Japan | Name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate Name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | Type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Kofun in Japan | Name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate Name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | Type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Shima City, Isobe-cho, Hazama)

The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.

Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | Type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Shima City, Isobe-cho, Hazama)
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== References == {{small|(Sources used in writing this article)}} * Publications by local governments: * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=History of Mie Prefecture|volume=Archaeological Materials 1|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=Preliminary Report on Tsukaana Kofun Excavation (Shima No. 4 Kofun)|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63081|title=Excavation Report for Shima City Sites, 2011–2016|publisher=Shima City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report (Shima City)|2018}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/material/files/group/57/shimashinobunkazai.pdf Cultural Properties of Shima City]}}|publisher=Shima City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Cultural Properties of Shima City|2018}}}} * Encyclopedias: * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Place names of Mie Prefecture|series={{ill|Japan Historical Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 24|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Heibonsha)|1983}}}}
== Related Literature == * {{Cite journal|ja|author=Yoshio Murakami|title=Stone Chambers in Shima|date=1976|journal=Shima Local History|publisher=Shima Local History Society|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Kansai University Archaeology Department|year=1992|chapter=Survey of Shima Kofun Cluster, Ago Town|title=Cultural History of the Kii Peninsula: Archaeological Section|publisher=Seibundo Publishing|ref=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | Type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Shima City, Isobe-cho, Hazama)

The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Shima City, Isobe-cho, Hazama)
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== References == {{small|(Sources used in writing this article)}} * Publications by local governments: * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=History of Mie Prefecture|volume=Archaeological Materials 1|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=Preliminary Report on Tsukaana Kofun Excavation (Shima No. 4 Kofun)|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63081|title=Excavation Report for Shima City Sites, 2011–2016|publisher=Shima City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report (Shima City)|2018}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/material/files/group/57/shimashinobunkazai.pdf Cultural Properties of Shima City]}}|publisher=Shima City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Cultural Properties of Shima City|2018}}}} * Encyclopedias: * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Place names of Mie Prefecture|series={{ill|Japan Historical Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 24|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Heibonsha)|1983}}}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | Type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Shima City, Isobe-cho, Hazama)
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== References == {{small|(Sources used in writing this article)}} * Publications by local governments: * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=History of Mie Prefecture|volume=Archaeological Materials 1|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=Preliminary Report on Tsukaana Kofun Excavation (Shima No. 4 Kofun)|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63081|title=Excavation Report for Shima City Sites, 2011–2016|publisher=Shima City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report (Shima City)|2018}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/material/files/group/57/shimashinobunkazai.pdf Cultural Properties of Shima City]}}|publisher=Shima City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Cultural Properties of Shima City|2018}}}} * Encyclopedias: * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Place names of Mie Prefecture|series={{ill|Japan Historical Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 24|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Heibonsha)|1983}}}}
== Related Literature == * {{Cite journal|ja|author=Yoshio Murakami|title=Stone Chambers in Shima|date=1976|journal=Shima Local History|publisher=Shima Local History Society|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Kansai University Archaeology Department|year=1992|chapter=Survey of Shima Kofun Cluster, Ago Town|title=Cultural History of the Kii Peninsula: Archaeological Section|publisher=Seibundo Publishing|ref=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}

== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Shima City, Isobe-cho, Hazama)

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | Type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Shima City, Isobe-cho, Hazama)
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== References == {{small|(Sources used in writing this article)}} * Publications by local governments: * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=History of Mie Prefecture|volume=Archaeological Materials 1|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=Preliminary Report on Tsukaana Kofun Excavation (Shima No. 4 Kofun)|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63081|title=Excavation Report for Shima City Sites, 2011–2016|publisher=Shima City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report (Shima City)|2018}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/material/files/group/57/shimashinobunkazai.pdf Cultural Properties of Shima City]}}|publisher=Shima City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Cultural Properties of Shima City|2018}}}} * Encyclopedias: * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Place names of Mie Prefecture|series={{ill|Japan Historical Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 24|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Heibonsha)|1983}}}}
== Related Literature == * {{Cite journal|ja|author=Yoshio Murakami|title=Stone Chambers in Shima|date=1976|journal=Shima Local History|publisher=Shima Local History Society|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Kansai University Archaeology Department|year=1992|chapter=Survey of Shima Kofun Cluster, Ago Town|title=Cultural History of the Kii Peninsula: Archaeological Section|publisher=Seibundo Publishing|ref=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | Type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | Type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
