{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館}} (Shima City, Isobe-cho, Hazama)
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== References == {{small|(Sources used in writing this article)}} * Publications by local governments: * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=History of Mie Prefecture|volume=Archaeological Materials 1|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie History)|2005}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=Preliminary Report on Tsukaana Kofun Excavation (Shima No. 4 Kofun)|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63081|title=Excavation Report for Shima City Sites, 2011–2016|publisher=Shima City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report (Shima City)|2018}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/material/files/group/57/shimashinobunkazai.pdf Cultural Properties of Shima City]}}|publisher=Shima City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Cultural Properties of Shima City|2018}}}} * Encyclopedias: * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Place names of Mie Prefecture|series={{ill|Japan Historical Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 24|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Heibonsha)|1983}}}}
== Related Literature == * {{Cite journal|ja|author=Yoshio Murakami|title=Stone Chambers in Shima|date=1976|journal=Shima Local History|publisher=Shima Local History Society|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Kansai University Archaeology Department|year=1992|chapter=Survey of Shima Kofun Cluster, Ago Town|title=Cultural History of the Kii Peninsula: Archaeological Section|publisher=Seibundo Publishing|ref=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Copper bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as copper bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include copper bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of aqqroximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include coqqer bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of aqqroximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include coqqer bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of aqqroximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include coqqer bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.

Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}.

The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include coqqer bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of aqqroximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include coqqer bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of aqqroximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include coqqer bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. Artifacts recovered include coqqer bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of aqqroximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include coqqer bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of aqqroximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include coqqer bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of aqqroximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."

The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | image Caption = Burial mound and entrance to the stone chamber | Alternate name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | Latitude = 34|18|39.80 | Longitude = 136|53|20.00 | ISO = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 20m | Burial Facility = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | Artifacts = Coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | Construction Period = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historical Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historical Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Tangible Cultural Properties = | Notes = | Map = Japan Mie | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Tsukaana Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a burial mound located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a designated historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of aqqroximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, artifacts such as coqqer bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}.
Interestingly, artifacts from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was designated as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). * 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). * 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). * 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== Burial Facility == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
Artifacts recovered include coqqer bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period artifacts beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
