{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = 34|18|39.80 | coordinates = 136|53|20.00 | omitted = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter 20m | burial = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | burial goods = Co11er bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | built = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historic Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Mie | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a Kofun located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a Historic Site1 historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, burial goods such as co11er bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}.
Interestingly, burial goods from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was Historic Site1 as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). ** 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). ** 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). ** 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
burial goods recovered include co11er bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period burial goods beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = 34|18|39.80 | coordinates = 136|53|20.00 | omitted = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter 20m | burial = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (Takakurayama style) | burial goods = Co11er bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | built = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historic Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Mie | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a Kofun located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群}}, a Historic Site1 historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} in Ise City. Although looted, burial goods such as co11er bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli}}.
Interestingly, burial goods from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was Historic Site1 as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). ** 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). ** 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). ** 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
burial goods recovered include co11er bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period burial goods beneath the stone chamber.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = 34|18|39.80 | coordinates = 136|53|20.00 | omitted = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 20m | burial = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (Takakurayama style) | burial goods = Co11er bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | built = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historic Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Mie | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a Kofun located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} in Ise City. Although looted, burial goods such as co11er bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}.
Interestingly, burial goods from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was Historic Site1 as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). ** 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). ** 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). ** 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
burial goods recovered include co11er bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period burial goods beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}} (Shima City, Isobe-cho, Hazama)
== notes ==
{{reflist}}
== References == {{small|(Sources used in writing this article)}} * Publications by local governments: ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=History of Mie Prefecture|volume=Archaeological Materials 1|publisher=Mie Prefecture|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Mie History)|2005}}}} ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=Preliminary Report on Tsukaana Kofun Excavation (Shima No. 4 Kofun)|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63081|title=Excavation Report for Shima City Sites, 2011–2016|publisher=Shima City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Excavation Report (Shima City)|2018}}}} ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/material/files/group/57/shimashinobunkazai.pdf Historic Site of Shima City]}}|publisher=Shima City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Historic Site of Shima City|2018}}}} * Encyclopedias: ** {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Shima Kofun Cluster|title=Place names of Mie Prefecture|series={{ill|Japan Historical Place name Encyclopedia|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 24|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Shima Kofun Cluster (Heibonsha)|1983}}}}
== Related Literature == * {{Cite journal|ja|author=Yoshio Murakami|title=Stone Chambers in Shima|date=1976|journal=Shima Local History|publisher=Shima Local History Society|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Kansai University Archaeology Department|year=1992|chapter=Survey of Shima Kofun Cluster, Ago Town|title=Cultural History of the Kii Peninsula: Archaeological Section|publisher=Seibundo Publishing|ref=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}}

The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}.

The site was Historic Site1 as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). ** 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). ** 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). ** 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
burial goods recovered include co11er bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period burial goods beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} in Ise City. Although looted, burial goods such as co11er bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}.
Interestingly, burial goods from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was Historic Site1 as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). ** 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). ** 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). ** 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
burial goods recovered include co11er bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period burial goods beneath the stone chamber.

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = 34|18|39.80 | coordinates = 136|53|20.00 | omitted = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 20m | burial = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (Takakurayama style) | burial goods = Co11er bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | built = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historic Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Mie | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a Kofun located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} in Ise City. Although looted, burial goods such as co11er bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}.
Interestingly, burial goods from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was Historic Site1 as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). ** 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). ** 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). ** 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
burial goods recovered include co11er bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period burial goods beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = 34|18|39.80 | coordinates = 136|53|20.00 | omitted = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 20m | burial = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (Takakurayama style) | burial goods = Co11er bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | built = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historic Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Mie | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a Kofun located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} in Ise City. Although looted, burial goods such as co11er bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}.
Interestingly, burial goods from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was Historic Site1 as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). ** 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). ** 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). ** 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
burial goods recovered include co11er bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period burial goods beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}} (Shima City, Isobe-cho, Hazama)
== notes ==

Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} in Ise City. Although looted, burial goods such as co11er bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}.
Interestingly, burial goods from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was Historic Site1 as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). ** 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). ** 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). ** 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
burial goods recovered include co11er bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period burial goods beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} in Ise City. Although looted, burial goods such as co11er bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}.
Interestingly, burial goods from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was Historic Site1 as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). ** 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). ** 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). ** 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
burial goods recovered include co11er bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period burial goods beneath the stone chamber.

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = 34|18|39.80 | coordinates = 136|53|20.00 | omitted = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 20m | burial = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (Takakurayama style) | burial goods = Co11er bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | built = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historic Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Mie | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a Kofun located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} in Ise City. Although looted, burial goods such as co11er bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}.
Interestingly, burial goods from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was Historic Site1 as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). ** 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). ** 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). ** 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
burial goods recovered include co11er bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period burial goods beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = 34|18|39.80 | coordinates = 136|53|20.00 | omitted = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 20m | burial = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (Takakurayama style) | burial goods = Co11er bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | built = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historic Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Mie | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a Kofun located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} in Ise City. Although looted, burial goods such as co11er bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}.
Interestingly, burial goods from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was Historic Site1 as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). ** 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). ** 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). ** 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
burial goods recovered include co11er bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period burial goods beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}} (Shima City, Isobe-cho, Hazama)
== notes ==

== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). ** 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). ** 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). ** 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
burial goods recovered include co11er bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period burial goods beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = 34|18|39.80 | coordinates = 136|53|20.00 | omitted = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 20m | burial = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (Takakurayama style) | burial goods = Co11er bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | built = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historic Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Mie | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a Kofun located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} in Ise City. Although looted, burial goods such as co11er bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}.
Interestingly, burial goods from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was Historic Site1 as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). ** 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). ** 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). ** 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
burial goods recovered include co11er bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period burial goods beneath the stone chamber.

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = 34|18|39.80 | coordinates = 136|53|20.00 | omitted = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 20m | burial = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (Takakurayama style) | burial goods = Co11er bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | built = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historic Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Mie | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a Kofun located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} in Ise City. Although looted, burial goods such as co11er bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}.
Interestingly, burial goods from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was Historic Site1 as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). ** 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). ** 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). ** 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
burial goods recovered include co11er bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period burial goods beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft}} Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = 34|18|39.80 | coordinates = 136|53|20.00 | omitted = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 20m | burial = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (Takakurayama style) | burial goods = Co11er bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | Subsidiary Tumuli = | built = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historic Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Mie | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a Kofun located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|12=draft}}, a Historic Site1 historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} in Ise City. Although looted, burial goods such as co11er bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}.
Interestingly, burial goods from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was Historic Site1 as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). ** 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). ** 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). ** 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
burial goods recovered include co11er bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period burial goods beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}} (Shima City, Isobe-cho, Hazama)

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 塚穴古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = 志島4号墳 |所属 = [[志島古墳群]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[志摩市]]阿児町志島234ほか |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 18|緯度秒 = 39.80 |経度度 = 136|経度分 = 53|経度秒 = 20.00 |ISO = JP-24 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径20m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]（高倉山型） |出土品 = 銅鋺・金銅製品・耳環・武器・馬具・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 志摩市指定史跡「志島古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Mie |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 塚穴古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''塚穴古墳'''（つかあなこふん、志島4号墳）は、[[三重県]][[志摩市]]阿児町志島にある古墳。[[志島古墳群]]（志摩市指定史跡）を構成する古墳の1つ。形状は[[円墳]]。
== 概要 == 三重県東部、[[志摩半島]]先端部の安乗崎・大王崎の間、緩やかに東に突出する海食台地の、北東端の段崖上に築造された古墳である。[[2012年|2012]]-[[2014年]]度（[[平成]]24-26年度）に発掘調査が実施されている。
墳形は円形で、直径約20メートルを測る。墳丘周囲には周溝が巡らされる{{Sfn|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}。ただし、北側では周溝は別の円弧を描いており、削平された5号墳と周溝を共有した可能性がある{{Sfn|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南南西方向に開口する。石室全長10.6メートルを測る大型石室で、[[伊勢市]]の[[高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|高倉山古墳]]に類似する高倉山型石室になる。石室内は盗掘に遭っているが、調査では銅鋺・金銅製品のほか耳環・武器・馬具・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]など多数の副葬品が検出されている。築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]中葉ごろと推定される{{Sfn|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}。なお、石室下層では古墳時代中期の[[5世紀]]前半（TK216型式期）の須恵器・土師器が検出されており、中期古墳を壊して築造された可能性があるとして注目される。
古墳域は[[1971年]]（[[昭和]]46年）に阿児町指定史跡（現在は志摩市指定史跡）に指定されている（史跡「志島古墳群」のうち）。
== 遺跡歴 == * [[1971年]]（昭和46年）3月31日、阿児町指定史跡に指定（史跡「志島古墳群」のうち、現在は志摩市指定史跡）。 * 石室実測調査（村上喜雄、[[1976年]]に報告）。 * 石室実測調査（[[関西大学]]文学部考古学研究室、[[1992年]]に報告）。 * [[2012年|2012]]-[[2014年]]度（[[平成]]24-26年度）、発掘調査（志摩市教育委員会、[[2018年]]に概要報告）{{Sfn|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}。 ** 2012年度（平成24年度）、墳丘測量・発掘調査：第1次調査。 ** 2013年度（平成25年度）、石室前庭部・石室内発掘調査：第2次調査。 ** 2014年度（平成26年度）、墳丘断割・周溝確認調査：第3次調査。
== 埋葬施設 == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南南西方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}。 * 石室全長：10.8メートル * 玄室：長さ7.7メートル、幅2.2メートル、高さ2.3メートル * 羨道：幅1.4メートル 玄室の奥壁では、最下段に大石2石を置いた上に石を積み上げるが、奥壁と側壁の境界はL字状の石を架け渡すなど不明瞭である。天井石は6石で、奥壁から2石目を最高所として開口部に向かって下がる{{Sfn|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}。石室の形態としては、長大な玄室・短小な羨道・断面弧状の玄室天井・両袖の退化などの点で[[高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|高倉山古墳]]（[[伊勢市]]）・小金3号墳（[[明和町 (三重県)|明和町]]）などと同様の特徴を有しており、「高倉山型石室」と捉えられる。
石室内は盗掘に遭っているが、石室内の調査では銅鋺・蛇尾・鋳造鈴・鍛造鈴・板状金銅製品・花形飾金具のほか、耳環・玉（空玉・勾玉・丸玉・小玉・トンボ玉）・刀・鏃・弓飾金具・刀子・刀子装具・銀装鉄釘・鉄釘・馬具（雲珠・双脚飾鋲・飾金具・鉸具・鉄製壺鐙・兵庫鎖）・須恵器（坏・高坏・平瓶・𤭯・甕）・土師器（坏・甕）などが検出されている。また石室内の断ち割り調査では、下層から古墳時代中期の須恵器（𤭯2・壺1）・土師器（高坏5）が検出されており、築造以前に中期古墳が存在した可能性が指摘される{{Sfn|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}。 <gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|開口部 </gallery>
== 関連施設 == * [[志摩市歴史民俗資料館]]（志摩市磯部町迫間）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=志島古墳群|title=三重県史|volume=資料編 考古1|publisher=三重県|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|志島古墳群（三重県史）|2005}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=塚穴古墳（志島古墳群4号墳）発掘調査概報|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/63081|title=平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|series=志摩市埋蔵文化財調査報告5|publisher=志摩市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|平成23～28年度 志摩市内遺跡発掘調査報告|2018}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://www.city.shima.mie.jp/material/files/group/57/shimashinobunkazai.pdf 志摩市の文化財]}}|publisher=志摩市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|志摩市の文化財|2018}}}} - リンクは志摩市ホームページ。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=志島古墳群|title=三重県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]24|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490247|ref={{Harvid|志島古墳群（平凡社）|1983}}}}
== 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=村上喜雄|title=志摩の横穴式石室|date=1976|journal=郷土志摩|publisher=志摩郷土会|volume=|number=49|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[関西大学]]文学部考古学研究室|author=|year=1992|chapter=阿児町志島古墳群の調査|title=紀伊半島の文化史的研究|volume=考古学編|series=関西大学文学部考古学研究第6冊|publisher=清文堂出版|isbn=|ref=}}
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Tsukaana Kofun (Shima)}} * [[志島古墳群]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:つかあなこふん}} [[Category:三重県の古墳]] [[Category:志摩市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:三重県内の市町村指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[塚穴古墳 (志摩市)]] to [[Tsukaana Kofun (Shima City)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tsukaana Kofun | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Shima No. 4 Kofun | Cluster = {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]] Ago-cho, Shima 234 and surrounding areas, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = 34|18|39.80 | coordinates = 136|53|20.00 | omitted = JP-24 | type = {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 20m | burial = Double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Takakurayama style) | burial goods = Co11er bowls, gilt-bronze objects, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | Subsidiary Tumuli = | built = Mid-7th century | Occupant = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Included in Shima City Designated Historic Site "Shima Kofun Cluster" | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Mie | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳, also known as Shima No. 4 Kofun) is a Kofun located in Ago-cho, Shima City, Mie Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shijima Kofun Cluster|lt=Shima Kofun Cluster|ja|志島古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a Historic Site1 historical site of Shima City. The tumulus is a {{ill|Round barrow|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round barrow|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
== Overview == Tsukaana Kofun is located on a marine terrace between Cape Anori and Cape Daiō, at the northeastern edge of the terrace cliff. Archaeological investigations were conducted between 2012 and 2014.
The tumulus is circular with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, surrounded by a moat. On the northern side, the moat arcs differently, suggesting it might have been shared with the now-erased No. 5 Kofun. The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. Measuring 10.6 meters in total length, the chamber is categorized as the Takakurayama type, similar to the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|qq=}} in Ise City. Although looted, burial goods such as co11er bowls, gilt-bronze items, earrings, weapons, horse fittings, and pottery have been recovered. The construction dates to the mid-7th century, the {{ill|End of the Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/End of the Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}.
Interestingly, burial goods from the early Kofun period (early 5th century) were found beneath the chamber floor, suggesting an earlier mound was dismantled to build this tumulus.
The site was Historic Site1 as a municipal historical site in 1971 as part of the "Shima Kofun Cluster."
== Excavation History == * '''1971''': Designated as a municipal historical site by Ago Town (now Shima City). * Stone chamber surveys were conducted by Yoshio Murakami (reported in 1976) and Kansai University's Department of Archaeology (reported in 1992). * '''2012-2014''': Excavations by the Shima City Board of Education (summary published in 2018). ** 2012: Mound survey and first excavation (Phase 1). ** 2013: Forecourt and chamber excavation (Phase 2). ** 2014: Cross-sectional survey of the mound and moat verification (Phase 3).
== burial == The burial facility is a double-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber opening to the south-southwest. Measurements are as follows: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 7.7 meters, width 2.2 meters, height 2.3 meters * Corridor: Width 1.4 meters
The chamber exhibits distinct features of the Takakurayama style, including an elongated main chamber, short corridor, arched chamber ceiling, and reduced double-sleeve layout. Similarities are noted with the {{ill|Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|ja|高倉山古墳 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakurayama Kofun (Ise)|qq=}} and Ogane No. 3 Kofun in Meiwa Town.
burial goods recovered include co11er bowls, jewelry (such as magatama and glass beads), weapons, horse fittings, and pottery. Stratigraphic analysis indicates earlier Kofun-period burial goods beneath the stone chamber.
<gallery> Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward back wall) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), sendou.jpg|Corridor (toward entrance) Tsukaana Kofun (Shima), entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
== Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|ja|志摩市歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima City Historical and Folklore Museum|qq=|1=Shima City Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11490924}} (Shima City, Isobe-cho, Hazama)
== notes ==
